techniques.name: Forehand Drive
techniques.description: The fundamental attacking stroke. A forward brushing motion that produces topspin and consistent speed.
key_points: Start with racket below table level
Rotate hips and waist for power
Brush the ball at its peak
common_mistakes: Using only arm strength without body rotation
Hitting the ball too late or too early
Racket angle too open or too closed

techniques.name: Backhand Drive
techniques.description: A compact topspin stroke from the backhand side, essential for rally play.
key_points: Contact ball in front of body

techniques.name: Push
techniques.description: A controlled backspin stroke used to return short balls and control rallies.
key_points: Keep the ball low over the net
common_mistakes: Pushing too high over the net

techniques.name: Forehand Loop
techniques.description: A powerful topspin attack that lifts and accelerates the ball with heavy rotation.
key_points: Full body rotation from legs to arm
common_mistakes: Hitting through the ball instead of brushing

techniques.name: Backhand Flick
techniques.description: An aggressive wrist-driven attack against short balls on the backhand side.
key_points: Step in close to the table
Contact ball at the top of the bounce
common_mistakes: Standing too far from the table

techniques.name: Counter Loop
techniques.description: Redirecting an opponent's topspin attack with your own topspin, played close to the table.
common_mistakes: Racket angle too open against topspin

techniques.name: Backspin Serve
techniques.description: A fundamental serve with backspin that keeps the ball low and forces a push return.
key_points: Brush under the ball with an open racket
Keep the serve low over the net

techniques.name: Pendulum Serve
techniques.description: A versatile serve where the racket swings like a pendulum, allowing various spin combinations.
key_points: Swing racket from right to left (for right-handers)
Vary contact point for different spins

techniques.name: Reverse Pendulum Serve
techniques.description: The opposite motion of the pendulum serve, swinging from left to right for different spin angles.
key_points: Swing racket from left to right

techniques.name: Block
techniques.description: A passive stroke that uses the opponent's energy to redirect the ball with control.
common_mistakes: Racket angle not adjusted for spin

techniques.name: Chop
techniques.description: A defensive backspin stroke played away from the table to return powerful attacks.
key_points: Keep the ball low and deep
common_mistakes: Standing too close to the table

techniques.name: Lob
techniques.description: A high, deep defensive shot with heavy topspin used as a last resort against smashes.
key_points: Hit ball high with heavy topspin
Aim deep to the opponent's end line
common_mistakes: Insufficient topspin to bring ball down

techniques.name: Side-to-Side Steps
techniques.description: Basic lateral movement to cover the table width efficiently.
key_points: Stay on the balls of your feet
Maintain a low center of gravity
Return to ready position after each shot

techniques.name: Pivot Footwork
techniques.description: Stepping around the backhand corner to use a forehand from the backhand side.
key_points: Quick step with the right foot (right-handers)
Recover quickly to cover forehand side

techniques.name: Cross-Step
techniques.description: A large lateral movement using a crossover step to reach wide balls.
key_points: Cross back foot behind or in front
